---
canvas:
  title: "Syllabus & Statics Quiz"
  quiz_type: assignment
  published: true
  shuffle_answers: true
  show_correct_answers: true
  allowed_attempts: -1
  time_limit: 20
  due_at: "2026-09-15T23:59:00Z"
  one_question_at_a_time: true
---

:::: {.question name="Stress Definition"}

Axial stress $\sigma$ is defined as which ratio?

- [x] Force divided by cross-sectional area, $F/A$
  - Correct — stress is force per unit area.
- [ ] Area divided by force, $A/F$
- [ ] Force times area, $F \cdot A$
- [ ] Change in length over original length

::::

:::: {.question name="Units of Stress"}

Which unit is equivalent to one pascal?

- [ ] $\text{N} \cdot \text{m}$
- [x] $\text{N/m}^2$
- [ ] $\text{kg} \cdot \text{m/s}$
- [ ] $\text{J/s}$

::::

:::: {.question name="Hooke Region" points=2}

In which region does $\sigma = E\varepsilon$ hold?

::: {.answer correct=true comment="Yes — linear elastic behaviour."}
The **linear-elastic** region, below the proportional limit.
:::

::: {.answer comment="No — that is past yielding."}
The plastic region.
:::

::: {.answer}
The fracture region.
:::

::::

:::: {.question type="essay_question" name="Ductile vs Brittle"}

In your own words, explain why a ductile material (e.g. mild steel) fails
differently from a brittle material (e.g. cast iron). Mention the stress–strain
curve and necking.

::::
